title: BitSight Current Ratings API - Security Rating Retrieval Conventions
applies_to: Portfolio current rating extraction for downstream reporting

confirmed_facts.fact: The Current Ratings endpoint is GET https://api.bitsighttech.com/ratings/v1/current-ratings.
Current Ratings provides a 24-hour snapshot of a company's rating.
The Current Ratings response includes pagination links, a count, a summaries.rating_as_of date, and result objects that include guid, name, rating, and rating_category.
BitSight recommends using pagination parameters such as limit and offset where available, and paginated responses can include next, next_url, previous, and count.
BitSight query parameters include limit, offset, fields, format, q, sort, start_date, end_date, and cursor, although parameter availability varies by endpoint.
The response codes for Current Ratings include 200, 401 for invalid token, 403 for permission denied, and 404 for not found.

implementation_notes: Treat current-ratings as the primary BitSight portfolio ratings feed for security-rating reporting.
Persist rating_as_of alongside every extracted rating row so reports can distinguish observation date from extraction date.
Use guid as the stable entity key and name only as a display label.
If the endpoint supports fields selection in your tenant, limit the response to the columns required by the downstream report.
Handle pagination until links.next or next_url is empty rather than assuming a single response page.
caveats: Current Ratings is a current-state snapshot and not a full historical ratings series.
Parameter availability can vary by endpoint, so not every generic BitSight query parameter should be assumed to work here without verification.
Access to ratings data is tied to BitSight product entitlements and portfolio visibility.
